# breeze operator update-all restarts every daemon this machine's discovery
# registry knows about, fanned out across repos — not just the one directory a
# single `daemon restart` targets. XDG_CACHE_HOME is shared across this whole
# script so both daemons register into the same registry.json.
env XDG_CACHE_HOME=$WORK/cache

env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/repo1/state
exec breeze ping
stdout 'pong'
exec breeze lock acquire canary-file --as alice --ttl 30m
cp stdout repo1-lock-id.txt

env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/repo2/state
exec breeze ping
stdout 'pong'

# update-all doesn't care which BREEZE_DIR the invoking process resolves to —
# its targets come entirely from the registry, not the caller's own directory.
exec breeze operator update-all
stdout 'restarted in place \(dir \S+/repo1/state\)'
stdout 'restarted in place \(dir \S+/repo2/state\)'

# State survived the fan-out restart for repo1, same as a single `daemon restart`.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/repo1/state
exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"holder":\s*"alice"'

env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/repo2/state
exec breeze stop
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/repo1/state
exec breeze stop
